Send only unified diffs instead of full files (up to 20-100× fewer tokens), and read large files with targeted `grep`/`sed` instead of full reads (up to 500×). Kernel-enforced path confinement hard-locks the agent to a configured root: no accidental reads or writes outside scope. Standalone for your file access or embed in any MCP server (Rust, Node.js, Python via WASM).

entries.name: SonAIengine/graph-tool-call
entries.description: [![graph-tool-call MCP server](https://glama.ai/mcp/servers/SonAIengine/graph-tool-call/badges/score.svg)](https://glama.ai/mcp/servers/SonAIengine/graph-tool-call) 🐍 🏠 - When tool count exceeds LLM context limits, accuracy collapses (248 tools → 12%). graph-tool-call builds a tool graph from OpenAPI/MCP specs and retrieves multi-step workflows via hybrid search (BM25 + graph traversal + embedding), recovering accuracy to 82% with 79% fewer tokens. Zero dependencies. Also works as an MCP Proxy — aggregate multiple MCP servers behind 3 meta-tools.

entries.name: 5g-ddos-mcp
entries.description: Connects to a server that detects 5G DDoS attacks using a specified dataset. Enables querying the server for attack data, monitoring its status, and potentially executing actions based on the data. Best suited for agents focused on network security and threat detection.

entries.description: Connects to a server that performs accessibility testing and bypasses Cloudflare using Camoufox and axe-core. Enables querying the status of loading errors, updating error attributes, and monitoring script loading. Best suited for agents that need to automate accessibility testing workflows and handle script loading issues.

entries.description: Connects to a server that provides accessibility analysis and remediation guidance for web components. Enables querying component properties, generating accessibility fixes via WAIA-ARIA patterns, and reviewing code for WCAG compliance. Best suited for agents focused on web development and accessibility testing.

Enables querying the codebase for features, searching for related code, and monitoring code changes. Best suited for agents that need to understand and reason about large software projects.
